plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-368 RP12.P02.M03.S01-RP12.P02.M04.S18 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-368 implements descriptor-only coverage of the RP12.P02 implementation slice: the full M03 Primary Implementation Slice model cycle (the twenty model rows plus documentation entry and index export check) and the M04 Secondary Workflow Slice head (package directory layout through Hermes model summary) as two descriptor-only sections over 40 planned rows with Hermes evidence (H12), Claude review packet (C12), and CP00-369 handoff, without opening billing, proforma, invoice, or write-down runtime or any other closed runtime surface.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-368 is a descriptor-only billing-core pack (RP12.P02.M03.S01-RP12.P02.M04.S18, risk B, 40 units) and is internally consistent across closeout-pack-plan.json, manifest.json, command-evidence.json, registry.js, service.js, validators.js, contracts/billing-core-contract.json, model.test.js, and README.md. The pack-level claim is correctly scoped: manifest production_ready=false, implementation_layer=descriptor, runtime_ready=false, flag billing_core_p02_implementation_slice_descriptor_verified — no production/runtime overclaim. The Hermes production_ready_candidate=true is a descriptor coverage/slice-valid flag distinct from pack-level production readiness, and is backed by validateBillingCoreCp368Coverage/validateBillingCoreCp368P02ImplementationSliceDescriptor and three CP368 tests asserting exactly 40 units (M03=22, M04=18), descriptor_only rows, and authority boundaries. No-write/no-real-data boundaries are intact: the billing source has no writeFile/network/child_process/fs-write calls (validator uses read-only readFile), and every guard flag (writes_product_state, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt, loads_real_fixture_data, cross_tenant_access, state writes, audit writes) is false/blocked in service.js, validators.js, and the contract. Claude remains a read-only reviewer and not final approver: claude_is_final_approval=false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true, allowed_tools=Read/Grep/Glob, and the CP178+ hardening blockers (failed_authentication_run, zero_byte_output, malformed_json_output, fenced_json_output, tool_call_shaped_output, disabled_tools_run, missing_raw_output_reference, missing_source_inspection_basis, claude_final_approval_claim, source_mutation_claim, production_enterprise_trust_claim) are enumerated; the readiness auth-preflight passed (exit 0, valid JSON). Hermes patterns are used only as a deterministic descriptor evidence-packet/receipt shape (receipt_shape ...no_write, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false), not embedded product runtime. The post-review evidence artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are correctly absent at this pre-review stage per the documented hardening sequence (status implemented_pending_review, pack_level_claude_review.status pending) — expected, not a defect. Only one cosmetic P3 finding (an unused local variable in the test) was identified; it does not affect correctness, validation evidence (npm test / npm run validate recorded exit 0), or the descriptor-verified claim. No P0/P1/P2 issues; closeout is not blocked.

findings.title: Unused local variable left in CP00-368 descriptor-only test
findings.details: In the test "CP00-368 p02 implementation slice rows stay descriptor-only", the line `const mspot = caseSet.sections["RP12.P02.M03"].rows;` assigns a value that is never referenced afterward (the following statement asserts descriptor.authority_boundary.claude_is_final_approval). It appears to be leftover scaffolding copied from the per-row loop above. It is harmless: command-evidence.json records `npm test` and `npm run validate` at exit_code 0, so it does not affect test correctness, the 40-unit coverage assertions, the descriptor-only guarantees, or the pack-level production_ready=false (descriptor-verified) claim, and it touches no boundary (no writes, no real data, no Claude authority change).
findings.recommendation: Remove the dead `mspot` assignment (or convert it into a real assertion) the next time this test file is edited. Non-blocking; safe to defer to a later pack.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking cosmetic nit. Does not block CP00-368 pack or goal closeout; recommend cleanup in a future touch of packages/billing/test/model.test.js.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-368 billing core p02 implementation slice descriptor pack
